title: MD
biography: Donald Ainslie Henderson was an American physician and epidemiologist who led the World Health Organization's global smallpox eradication campaign from 1966 to 1977, achieving the only complete eradication of a human disease in history. He later served as Dean of the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health and became a leading advisor on bioterrorism preparedness following the September 11 attacks.
aiSummary: D.A. Henderson was an epidemiology and public health specialist with an H-index of 50 at the World Health Organization and Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health. Led the WHO global smallpox eradication campaign (1966-1977), achieving the only eradication of a human disease. Based in United States.
techniquesInvented: Ring vaccination and surveillance-containment strategy for smallpox eradication
education: University of Rochester School of Medicine (MD, 1954)
Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health (MPH, 1960)

affiliations.hospitalName: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health

timeline.title: Appointed head of the WHO Smallpox Eradication Programme
Last natural case of smallpox recorded in Somalia, marking the end of the disease
WHO officially certified the global eradication of smallpox
Became Dean of the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
